title: Lappula macrantha
intro: Lappula macrantha, also known as the large-flowered Lappula or large-flowered stickseed, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the family Boraginaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Lappula macrantha include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Lappula macrantha is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
taxonomy: Lappula macrantha is classified in the family Boraginaceae and genus Lappula. It belongs to the kingdom Plantae. Further, it is classified as a species, Lappula macrantha, within the genus Lappula. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and evolutionary relationships. Lappula macrantha's classification is part of the larger taxonomy of the plant kingdom.
